request.question: How many venues are involved in the main tournament which is part of the English Cricket Team's 2017-2018 schedule?

title: 2017–18 Ashes series
Betty Archdale

English cricket team in Australia in 2017–18
sentences: The 2017–18 Ashes series is a forthcoming series of Test cricket matches to be contested between England and Australia for The Ashes.
The series will be played at five venues across Australia between 23 November 2017 and 8 January 2018.
Helen Elizabeth "Betty" Archdale (21 August 1907 – 11 January 2000) was an educationalist and cricketer.
She was a captain of the English women's cricket team in 1934 and 1935.
In 1934/35 she led the first English cricket team to tour Australia and New Zealand, the result of which was a 2-0 victory over Australia.
This tour did much both to raise the status of women's cricket and to heal some of the damage done to Anglo-Australian cricket relations by bodyline two years earlier.
Centenary Test refers to two matches of Test cricket played between the English cricket team and the Australian cricket team, the first in 1977 and the second in 1980.
These matches were played to mark the 100th anniversaries of the first Test cricket matches played in Australia (1877) and in England (1880) respectively.
Neither match was played for The Ashes.
Robert "Bob" Elliott Storey Wyatt (2 May 1901, Milford Heath House, Surrey, England – 20 April 1995, Treliske, Truro, Cornwall) was an English cricket player.
He played for Warwickshire, Worcestershire, and the English cricket team.
Before the beginning of the 2005 English cricket season began, a resurgent English cricket team had won four Test series in a row, going unbeaten through the 2004 calendar year.
The start of the international season saw England defeat Bangladesh 2–0 in their two-match series, winning both Tests by an innings.
This was followed by a tri-nations one-day tournament that also featured Australia.
However, all eyes were on the Ashes.
Australia still started as favourites, but England, for the first time in years, were expected to put up a real challenge to Aussie supremacy.
The English cricket team toured India during February, March and April 2006.

The English cricket team was aspiring to maintain the form that took them to second place in the ICC Test Championship before their disastrous spell against Pakistan, and which helped win the 2005 Ashes series at home to Australia.

This goal was substantially hindered by the usual stomach complaints which nearly always dog the English team in the opening weeks of Indian tours, and a recurrence of an injury to the captain Michael Vaughan; the swing bowler Simon Jones and the absence of Ashley Giles who missed the tour for an operation.

As well as this, stand-in captain Marcus Trescothick flew home for "personal reasons", not wishing to divulge further, leaving Andrew Flintoff, who missed the birth of his son, to take on the title of skipper for the first time having to captain two maiden international cricketers on the English side: Alastair Cook and Monty Panesar as well as Shanthakumaran Sreesanth, Piyush Chawla and Munaf Patel for the home team.

International cricket played in the 2006 cricket season is defined as matches scheduled between May and August 2006 in all cricketing countries, as well as all international matches scheduled for the 2006 English cricket season.
Matches between January and April are defined as belonging to the 2005–06 season, while matches between September and December are defined as the 2006–07 season.

The main matches in this period were played in England, as this was in the middle of the English cricket season, but the third edition of the ICC Intercontinental Cup is defined as a part of the 2006 season, even though the tournament will stretch into February 2007, and three A teams are also scheduled to tour the northwestern part of Australia in June and July 2006.
The Barmy Army is a semi-organised group of English cricket fans which arranges touring parties for some of its members to follow the English cricket team on overseas tours.
The name is also applied to followers of the team who join in with match day activities in the crowd, but do not necessarily travel as part of an organised tour.
The 2004 English cricket season saw the English cricket team record a 4-0 Test series whitewash over the West Indies and a comfortable 3-0 win over New Zealand.
Their one-day form was sporadic, however.
In the Natwest Trophy, they failed to make the final, which saw New Zealand defeat the West Indies by 107 runs.
In the Natwest Challenge, they beat India 2-1.
In domestic cricket, Warwickshire won the County Championship.
The England cricket team is scheduled to tour Australia between November 2017 and February 2018 to play five Tests and five One Day Internationals (ODIs).
They will also take part in a three nation Twenty20 International (T20I) tournament, along with New Zealand, who will co-host the tournament along with Australia.
The Test series will form part of the 2017–18 Ashes series.
In May 2017, it was confirmed that the WACA Ground would host the Test and ODI in Perth, as the planned new ground was unable to open in time.

title: English cricket team in Australia in 2017–18
2017–18 Ashes series
2017–18 Ashes series

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Produce a compact, structured first-hop summary that helps the next agent fetch the missing second-hop facts. The summary must be one labeled block with these four short sections (each 1–4 lines):

1) Evidence found — For each concrete target term or name mentioned in the passages, list the target and which passage(s) mention it, and quote a short verbatim excerpt (≤15 words) or any exact numeric/date token from the passage. Preserve the quoted text and any numeric/date formatting exactly as it appears in the passages. Include passage identifier(s) exactly as given (title or index).

2) Ambiguities — Enumerate any names or terms that correspond to multiple candidate pages or where the passages return multiple variants or conflicting values. For each ambiguous item, list the candidate labels or note the conflict using only tokens from the passages where possible.

3) Bridge / missing fact — State exactly which relation(s) or fact(s) needed to answer the question are not present or are unresolved in the provided passages (e.g., birth date, publication year, author of adaptation, founding year, award count). Be explicit and concise.

4) Retrieval direction — Provide 2–4 concrete next-hop retrievals: specific page titles or disambiguation pages, or precise search queries the next agent should run to fetch the missing bridge facts. Use actionable, precise phrasing (page title or quoted query). End this section with an explicit deferral of the final answer when the passages do not unambiguously supply all required facts.

Formatting and behavior rules:
- Produce only the single field `summary` as the output. The `summary` text must follow the four labeled sections above.
- Always quote verbatim evidence (names, short excerpts, dates, numbers) exactly as in the passages; do not reformat numeric/date tokens.
- Do NOT produce a final answer here unless every required fact to resolve the question is present and unambiguous in the provided passages (i.e., each named entity corresponds to a single candidate and the needed relation value is explicitly present). If facts are unambiguous and complete, you may state that and include a concise final conclusion in the `summary`; otherwise explicitly defer (for example: "Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets below.").
- If the natural summary would otherwise be a single bare token or name, append a one-line bridge clause that identifies the missing relation and at least one retrieval target rather than ending with the bare token.
- Keep phrasing concise and factual; favor short lines, source-linked evidence excerpts, and explicit next-step instructions. Do not attempt to fetch pages yourself—only list retrieval targets/queries.

Example structure to follow in the `summary` field (use passage text and titles exactly):
Evidence found: ...
Ambiguities: ...
Bridge / missing fact: ...
Retrieval direction: ...

End of instructions.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Required output format and constraints:
- Produce exactly one field named `query`. Its value must be a single short, keyword-style search string (no full sentences, no explanations, no assertions, no user-facing clarifications).
- The query must be terse (preferably <= 12 words) and contain no clause punctuation or sentence grammar (no question marks, no leading "who/what is", no verbs forming assertions).
- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.
- When the inputs present multiple entities or candidate items that must be linked (for example two titles, two names, or an anchor title plus a candidate), include both entity tokens from the inputs and a single neutral connector token between or adjacent to them to indicate the evidence bridge to retrieve (e.g., "EntityA EntityB director").
- If the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + "regular season") as part of the short query.
- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.
- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. (Downstream retrieval will be attempted first; user clarification is allowed only after retrieval fails.)

Produce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single text field only):
- Output exactly one concise summary string (no extra fields, labels, or metadata). Keep the summary to 1–2 short sentences unless the evidence forces a single-token identification (see below).
- Primary goal: integrate the first-hop identity in `context` with facts found in `passages` so the final answer agent has the explicit, supported linkage it needs.

When the question requires linking the context entity to information in the passages and the passages supply the bridging fact:
- Produce a concise (1–2 sentence) integrated summary that (1) names the context entity (using the form present in `context`), (2) names the supporting entity/attribute found in `passages` (using the form present in the inputs), and (3) states the explicit relation or fact (membership, creator attribution, partnership, founding/establishment, award, etc.) exactly as supported by the inputs. Use only facts present in `context` and `passages` and avoid introducing any unsupported details.

Name and attribution formatting:
- If a canonical multi-token name (given + family name or full official name) appears in the inputs, use that full form in the summary; do not truncate to a surname or partial name.
- For questions about creator/author/credit, prefer the person-name attribution as given in the inputs over returning the title of a work or event.

Identification requests:
- If the question explicitly asks for a single identification (for example, asks "which X" or otherwise requests a named token or label), return exactly the canonical name(s) as presented in the passages. If multiple valid names are requested, return them comma-separated. Do not add explanatory clauses, relational fragments, or extra text in these cases—only the canonical name(s).

When evidence is missing or ambiguous:
- If the passages do not supply a clear bridging fact, state that succinctly (e.g., "No supporting evidence in the provided passages" or "Unclear from the provided sources"). Retain uncertainty rather than asserting unsupported facts.

Conciseness and fidelity constraints:
- Keep the summary short and factual (1–2 sentences). Do not expand into long biographies, exhaustive lists, or unrelated details.
- Do not assert facts that are not present in `context` and `passages`.
- Avoid long verbatim copying of passage text, but short verbatim names or phrases necessary for fidelity are allowed.
- Treat the `context` field as the primary first-hop identity to be linked to `passages` when integrating evidence.

Produce the `summary` now based only on the supplied `question`, `context`, and `passages`.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Use summary_1 and summary_2 as the sole evidential basis for the final answer; give greatest weight to summary_2 (the later, post-retrieval integrated evidence). Do not introduce facts from outside these summaries.

Output rules (apply in order):

1) If summary_1 and summary_2 clearly and unambiguously agree on the same concise answer, set the value of `answer` to exactly one minimal string and nothing else:
   - For a binary yes/no conclusion, emit the single lowercase token: yes or no.
   - For any other concise label, emit that label exactly as it appears in the summaries (trim leading/trailing whitespace and strip any surrounding punctuation or wrapper syntax such as surrounding parentheses, quotes, or JSON-like braces). Do not add words, sentences, punctuation, quotes, brackets, or any other surrounding text.

2) Preserve multi-token labels: if any visible summary contains a multi-token form of the answer (for example a full personal name, a multi-word company or place), output that full multi-token form verbatim. Only shorten a label if no multi-token form appears in either summary.

3) Company/developer preference: when the question requests a company or developer and the summaries show a subordinate studio together with an explicit parent/publisher label (e.g., in parentheses or with phrasing like "published by"), choose the parent/publisher name as the single minimal answer string. If no parent/publisher is explicitly indicated, choose the best-supported developer/studio name from the summaries.

4) If the summaries conflict or are ambiguous, do NOT apply the unanimous-agreement rule in (1). Instead, use summary_2 as the primary basis and emit the minimal concise string supported by summary_2, applying rules (2) and (3) as relevant. If summary_2 provides no concise supported answer, set `answer` to the minimal explicit token "unknown".

Formatting constraints (must always be followed):
- Produce exactly one field: `answer`.
- The value of `answer` must be a single minimal string (no surrounding text, punctuation, explanation, or structural wrappers).
- Do not output extra fields, labels, or markup.

Additional guidance:
- If summary_1 describes an earlier unresolved bridge but summary_2 provides a resolved, supported conclusion, use the conclusion from summary_2.
- If a concise answer string can be drawn verbatim from the summaries, prefer that verbatim text over paraphrase.

End.

response.hop1_query: How many venues are involved in the main tournament which is part of the English Cricket Team's 2017-2018 schedule?
hop1_docs: English cricket team in Australia in 2017–18 | The England cricket team is scheduled to tour Australia between November 2017 and February 2018 to play five Tests and five One Day Internationals (ODIs).  They will also take part in a three nation Twenty20 International (T20I) tournament, along with New Zealand, who will co-host the tournament along with Australia.  The Test series will form part of the 2017–18 Ashes series.  In May 2017, it was confirmed that the WACA Ground would host the Test and ODI in Perth, as the planned new ground was unable to open in time.

2017–18 Regional Super50 | The 2017–18 Regional Super50 is scheduled to be the 44th edition of the Regional Super50, the domestic limited-overs cricket competition for the countries of the West Indies Cricket Board (WICB).  The tournament is scheduled to be held in early 2018, with the English county sides Kent and Hampshire being invited to take part in the competition.  Kent took part in the previous edition of the tournament.  Barbados are the defending champions.

2018 ICC World Cricket League Division Four | The 2018 ICC World Cricket League Division Four is an international cricket tournament that is scheduled to take place during mid-2018.  It will form part of the 2017–22 cycle of the World Cricket League (WCL) which determines the qualification for the 2023 Cricket World Cup.

2018 ICC World Cricket League Division Two | 2018 ICC World Cricket League Division Two is a cricket tournament that is scheduled to take place in 2018.  Oman and Canada were the top two teams in the 2017 ICC World Cricket League Division Three tournament in Uganda and were promoted as a result.  They will be joined by the bottom four teams from the ongoing 2015–17 ICC World Cricket League Championship to determine the final two spots in the 2018 Cricket World Cup Qualifier.

2017–18 Pac-12 Conference men's basketball season | The 2017–18 Pac-12 Conference men's basketball season begins with practices in October 2017 followed by the 2017–18 NCAA Division I men's basketball season in November 10, 2017 with the conference schedule will begin on December 27, 2017.  The 2018 Pac-12 Tournament in March 2018 at the T-Mobile Arena in Paradise, Nevada.  Due to the Pac-12's use of that venue for their conference tournament, the Pac-12 Tournament will take place one week earlier than usual, ending the week before Selection Sunday.  This could result in teams having nearly two weeks off before the 2018 NCAA tournament.  As a result, it is anticipated that the Pac-12 regular season will begin in late-December.  Pac-12 teams' first Pac-12 game would likely be on the weekend of December, 2017 with a second game on December , 2017.  Coaches have requested that Pac-12 game be scheduled between Christmas and New Year's Day, accordingly each team will play two conference games in early December before finishing non-conference play.

2017–18 Pro50 Championship | The 2017–18 Pro50 Championship is scheduled to be the fifteenth edition of the Pro50 Championship, a List A cricket tournament in Zimbabwe.  The competition will run from December 2017 to March 2018 and will include a new team, the Rising Stars, along with the existing four teams.  Matabeleland Tuskers are the defending champions.

2018 ICC Women's World Twenty20 | The 2018 ICC Women's World Twenty20 will be hosted in the West Indies from 2 to 25 November 2018, during the 2018–19 international cricket season.  It will be the sixth edition of the ICC Women's World Twenty20, and the second hosted by the West Indies (after the 2010 edition).  The tournament was awarded to the West Indies Cricket Board (WICB) at the 2013 annual conference of the International Cricket Council (ICC).  The tournament's dates were confirmed at an ICC board meeting in January 2015.  In February 2017, the ICC confirmed that this would be the first T20 tournament that uses the Umpire Decision Review System, with one review per side.  The qualifier tournament for the competition is scheduled to be held in late 2017 or early 2018.

- Fetch the tournament's page: "2017–18 Trans-Tasman Tri-Series" (or "2017–18 Tri-Nation Series") — check the "venues" or "Matches" section.

Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets above.
response.hop2_query: England New Zealand Australia three nation T20I tournament venues
hop2_docs: 2017–18 Trans-Tasman Tri-Series | The 2017–18 Trans-Tasman Tri-Series is an upcoming Twenty20 International (T20I) cricket tournament scheduled to be held in Australia and New Zealand in February 2018.  It is a tri-nation series between Australia, England and New Zealand.  It follows on from England's tour of Australia, which includes the 2017–18 Ashes series, and takes the place of the planned Chappell–Hadlee Trophy series.

English cricket team in Australia in 2017–18 | The England cricket team is scheduled to tour Australia between November 2017 and February 2018 to play five Tests and five One Day Internationals (ODIs).  They will also take part in a three nation Twenty20 International (T20I) tournament, along with New Zealand, who will co-host the tournament along with Australia.  The Test series will form part of the 2017–18 Ashes series.  In May 2017, it was confirmed that the WACA Ground would host the Test and ODI in Perth, as the planned new ground was unable to open in time.

2016 Rugby League Four Nations squads | The Four Nations Rugby Squad is a biennial rugby tournament representing the top three nations of the sport including an additional underperforming team.  Australia, England and New Zealand are the top three nations excelling in the game.  The event is recognized as Ladbroke Four Nations because the tournament is sponsored by Ladbrokes, a British gambling company.  The squads competing in the 2016 rugby tournament consists of Australia, England, New Zealand and Scotland.  The rosters for each squad below consist of player’s information and the head coach of each team.

2014 Rugby League Four Nations | The 2014 Rugby League Four Nations tournament was the fourth staging of the Rugby League Four Nations tournament played in Australia and New Zealand over three weeks from Saturday, 25 October to Saturday, 15 November 2014.  The series was contested by regular participants Australia, England and New Zealand, joined by Samoa, having won their Pacific qualifier against Fiji.  New Zealand won the tournament, defeating Australia in the final at Wellington's Westpac Stadium on Saturday 15 November.

List of England Twenty20 International cricketers | A Twenty20 International (T20I) is an international cricket match between two teams that have official T20I status, as determined by the International Cricket Council.  It is played under the rules of Twenty20 cricket and is the shortest form of the game.  The first such match was played on 17 February 2005 between Australia and New Zealand.  The England cricket team played its first T20I match on 13 June 2005, against Australia as part of Australia's 2005 Ashes tour, winning the match by 100 runs.

1999 Rugby League Tri-Nations | The 1999 Rugby League Tri-Nations series was contested by Australia, Great Britain and New Zealand in 1999.  It was the first multi-national rugby league tournament to feature the sport's three world powers since the 1989-92 World Cup (the 1995 World Cup featured England and Wales separately).  Co-hosts Australia and New Zealand played in the final, which was narrowly won by the Kangaroos.  Great Britain didn't win a game of the tournament.

New Zealand cricket team in England in 2013 | The New Zealand cricket team was in England from 4 May to 27 June 2013 for a tour consisting of two Test matches, three One Day Internationals and two Twenty20 International matches.  The New Zealand team also competed in the 2013 ICC Champions Trophy between the ODI series and T20I series.  The tour followed England's tour of New Zealand two months earlier.
